summary: Iteration 6 — Direct image upload feature. Backend: 10/10 pytest cases pass (auth-gated upload, valid PNG returns {url,filename,size} with /api/uploads/ path, file retrievable via GET, .txt rejected with 400 'Unsupported file type', >10MB rejected, all 6 allowed extensions accepted: jpg/jpeg/png/webp/gif/avif). Frontend: confirmed visually via screenshots — all four required data-testids exist and work end-to-end: album-upload-btn, blog-cover-upload, pkg-hero-upload, pkg-gallery-upload. UploadButton correctly shows 'Uploading...' loader state. Uploaded URL is the absolute backend URL (https://...preview.emergentagent.com/api/uploads/<uuid>.png) and renders as a preview thumbnail inside the editor. Package hero editor screenshot proves full-URL storage (visible in input field).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 760 lines — recommend modularizing (split routes into separate routers: auth, albums, packages, blog, uploads) for maintainability.

Upload endpoint reads entire file into memory before size check (await file.read()); for very large attempted uploads this allocates memory before the 10MB rejection. Consider streaming with a running byte counter that aborts early.

ALLOWED_EXT validation is by file extension only (no MIME magic-byte sniffing). A user could rename hello.txt → hello.png and the server would accept it. Low risk because StaticFiles serves with content-type inferred from extension, but worth noting.

UPLOAD_DIR.mkdir and StaticFiles mount happen AFTER app.include_router and AFTER CORS middleware addition. Works, but conventional FastAPI ordering places middleware and static mounts before include_router for clarity.
Storage is ephemeral container disk (already acknowledged in agent context). Recommend documenting this in README and planning S3/Cloudinary migration.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all required testids present, upload integration verified visually)

seed_data_creation: Tests upload disposable PNGs (in-memory generated) per run; files persist in /app/backend/uploads/ but are uniquely-named UUIDs and do not pollute admin UI.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend upload endpoint POST /api/admin/upload is fully tested and stable. Frontend UploadButton component is reused across Album/Blog/Package editors. Test creds in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. test_iter6_upload.py uses helper _png_bytes() to construct minimal valid PNG bytes; the test runner script encountered Playwright API 'wait_for_response' usage issue (use page.expect_response context manager instead) but visual screenshots confirmed all functionality. No retest required.
